docs.title: HALO Core Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to the HALO Core documentation. HALO (Holistic Agent Logical Orchestrator) Core is an intelligence workspace for teams that need to turn raw information into clear, usable outputs.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text: Audience Documentation End Users User Handbook Administrators Admin Guide Developers Technical Reference

docs.title: What HALO Core Does
docs.text:
• Collect sources — files, web findings, connector results
  • Ask questions — source-grounded AI chat
  • Generate outputs — reports, infographics, podcasts, presentations
  • Save insights — reusable notes from your work

docs.title: Team-Based AI Collaboration
docs.text: Run specialized AI agents with different roles, instructions, and tools.

docs.title: Smart Delegation
docs.text: Multiple coordination modes: direct, skill-based delegation, or team-wide collaboration.

docs.title: Source-Grounded Responses
docs.text: Answers are grounded in your selected documents with citations.

docs.title: Transparent Execution
docs.text: Inspect tool calls, agent actions, and reasoning traces.

docs.title: Studio Pipeline
docs.text: Generate reports, presentations, podcasts, and more from your research context.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Clone and setup
git clone https://github.com/aizech/halo_core.git
cd halo_core
python -m venv .venv
. .venv/Scripts/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Configure API key
echo 'OPENAI_API_KEY = "sk-..."' > .streamlit/secrets.toml

# Run
streamlit run app/main.py

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to HALO Core are documented on this page.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• DICOM Tools: Medical imaging anonymization page with HIPAA Safe Harbor compliant tag removal
  • Configurable anonymization (patient, institution, study data)
  • UID regeneration for Study/Series/SOP Instance
  • Private tag removal
  • Batch processing of multiple DICOM files
  • Export anonymization mapping as CSV
  • MCP Connectors: Environment variable configuration for external integrations
  • Notion API integration
  • Google Drive OAuth credentials
  • Microsoft 365 / OneDrive (MSAL authentication)
  • PACS Integration: Environment variables for DICOM PACS server connection
  • Auto-anonymization: Optional DICOM anonymization on upload to Sources

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Documentation restructured into three main sections: User Handbook, Admin Guide, Technical Reference
  • Mkdocs theme customized with corpusanalytica.com color palette (#00C2A8)
  • Developer documentation moved to docs/reference/ subdirectory

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Team-based AI agent orchestration with delegation strategies
  • Coordination modes: direct_only, delegate_on_complexity, always_delegate, coordinated_rag
  • Structured run telemetry for chat turns
  • Memory backend support via HALO_AGENT_DB
  • Streaming event normalization for tool calls
  • Knowledge retrieval with LanceDB vector search
  • Windows vector search fallback for FTS lock issues

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Migrated to Agno agent framework
  • Updated streaming to use RunEvent/TeamRunEvent enums
  • Improved chat runtime with fallback behavior

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Core Streamlit application with sidebar navigation
  • Sources panel for file upload and management
  • Chat panel with source-grounded responses
  • Studio panel for template-based output generation

docs.title: Configuration Layers
docs.text:
HALO Core uses multiple configuration layers:

  1. Environment variables — System-level settings
  2. Secrets file — Sensitive credentials
  3. Application config — User-configurable settings
  4. Agent configs — Per-agent behavior

docs.title: Setting Environment Variables
docs.text:
Windows PowerShell:

$env:OPENAI_API_KEY = "sk-..."
$env:HALO_DATA_DIR = "C:\data\halo"

Linux/macOS:

export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
export HALO_DATA_DIR="/data/halo"

Docker:

docker run -e OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... -e HALO_DATA_DIR=/data halo-core

docs.title: Secrets File Location
docs.text: .streamlit/secrets.toml

docs.title: Format
docs.text:
# API Keys
OPENAI_API_KEY = "sk-..."

# Optional
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = "sk-ant-..."
GOOGLE_API_KEY = "AI..."

# Memory Backend
HALO_AGENT_DB = "sqlite:///data/memory.db"

# Custom Data Directory
HALO_DATA_DIR = "data"

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text:
!!! warning Never commit .streamlit/secrets.toml to version control.

  1. Add to .gitignore: .streamlit/secrets.toml

  2. Use environment variables in production

  3. Rotate API keys regularly

  4. Restrict file permissions: bash chmod 600 .streamlit/secrets.toml

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: This guide covers infrastructure setup and deployment options for HALO Core.

docs.title: Deployment Options
docs.text: Option Use Case Complexity Local Development Development, testing Low Streamlit Cloud Demo, lightweight hosting Low Docker Container Production, portability Medium Cloud VM Full control, enterprise High

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text: Requirement Minimum Recommended Python 3.10 3.11+ RAM 4 GB 8 GB+ Disk 10 GB 50 GB+ CPU 2 cores 4+ cores

docs.title: Software Dependencies
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • FFmpeg (for audio/video processing)
  • Git (for version control)
  • Docker (for containerized deployment)

docs.title: 1. Clone Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/aizech/halo_core.git
cd halo_core

docs.title: 2. Create Virtual Environment
docs.text:
python -m venv .venv

# Windows PowerShell
. .venv/Scripts\Activate.ps1

# Linux/macOS
source .venv/bin/activate

docs.title: 3. Install Dependencies
docs.text:
pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: 4. Configure Secrets
docs.text:
Create .streamlit/secrets.toml:

OPENAI_API_KEY = "sk-..."

docs.title: 5. Run Application
docs.text: streamlit run app/main.py

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
• No persistent storage between sessions
  • Session timeout after inactivity (~15-20 min)
  • Cold starts may take seconds
  • No access to local files/env vars
  • User memory backend disabled

docs.title: Deployment Steps
docs.text:
1. Push code to GitHub repository
  2. Connect to Streamlit Cloud
  3. Deploy from repository
  4. Configure secrets in Streamlit Cloud dashboard

docs.title: Secrets Configuration
docs.text:
In Streamlit Cloud dashboard, add:

OPENAI_API_KEY = "sk-..."

docs.title: Dockerfile
docs.text:
FROM python:3.11-slim

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    ffmpeg \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy requirements and install
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy application
COPY . .

# Create data directory
RUN mkdir -p /app/data

# Expose port
EXPOSE 8501

# Run application
CMD ["streamlit", "run", "app/main.py", "--server.port=8501", "--server.address=0.0.0.0"]

docs.title: Build and Run
docs.text:
# Build image
docker build -t halo-core .

# Run container
docker run -p 8501:8501 \
  -e OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... \
  -v halo-data:/app/data \
  halo-core

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
version: '3.8'

services:
  halo-core:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8501:8501"
    environment:
      - OPENAI_API_KEY=${OPENAI_API_KEY}
      - HALO_DATA_DIR=/app/data
      - HALO_AGENT_DB=sqlite:///app/data/memory.db
    volumes:
      - halo-data:/app/data
      - ./secrets:/app/.streamlit:ro

volumes:
  halo-data:

docs.title: Setup Steps
docs.text:
1. Provision VM
  2. Choose appropriate instance size
  3. Configure security groups/firewall

  4. Set up SSH access

  5. Install Dependencies ```bash # Update system sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y

# Install Python sudo apt install python3.11 python3.11-venv -y

# Install FFmpeg sudo apt install ffmpeg -y ```

  1. Deploy Application bash git clone https://github.com/aizech/halo_core.git cd halo_core python3.11 -m venv .venv source .venv/bin/activate pip install -r requirements.txt

  2. Configure as Service

Create /etc/systemd/system/halo-core.service:

```ini [Unit] Description=HALO Core Application After=network.target

[Service] Type=simple User=halo WorkingDirectory=/opt/halo_core Environment="PATH=/opt/halo_core/.venv/bin" ExecStart=/opt/halo_core/.venv/bin/streamlit run app/main.py --server.port 8501 --server.address 0.0.0.0 Restart=always RestartSec=10

[Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target ```

Enable and start:

bash sudo systemctl daemon-reload sudo systemctl enable halo-core sudo systemctl start halo-core

  1. Configure Reverse Proxy

Using Nginx:

```nginx server { listen 80; server_name halo.example.com;

   location / {
       proxy_pass http://localhost:8501;
       proxy_http_version 1.1;
       proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
       proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
       proxy_set_header Host $host;
       proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
   }

} ```

docs.title: DICOM Configuration
docs.text: System configuration for DICOM anonymization and PACS integration.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
DICOM (Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine) configuration covers:

  • Auto-anonymization — Automatic PHI removal on upload
  • PACS integration — Connection to Picture Archiving and Communication Systems
  • Anonymization defaults — Default tag handling behavior

docs.title: Auto-Anonymization
docs.text:
# Enable automatic anonymization on upload to Sources
DICOM_ANONYMIZE_ON_UPLOAD=false

When enabled:

  • All DICOM files uploaded to Sources are anonymized automatically
  • Original files are not stored
  • Anonymization mapping is saved

docs.title: PACS Server Configuration
docs.text:
# DICOM PACS server connection (for MCP connector)
DICOM_PACS_HOST=
DICOM_PACS_PORT=
DICOM_PACS_AE_TITLE=
Variable Description DICOM_PACS_HOST PACS server hostname or IP DICOM_PACS_PORT PACS server port (typically 11112) DICOM_PACS_AE_TITLE Application Entity title for identification

docs.title: Default Tags
docs.text:
The following tags are anonymized by default (HIPAA Safe Harbor):

Patient Identifiers:

  • PatientName
  • PatientID
  • PatientBirthDate
  • PatientSex
  • PatientAge
  • PatientAddress
  • PatientWeight
  • PatientTelephoneNumbers
  • OtherPatientIDs
  • OtherPatientNames
  • EthnicGroup

Institution Identifiers:

  • InstitutionName
  • InstitutionAddress
  • ReferringPhysicianName
  • PhysiciansOfRecord
  • PerformingPhysicianName
  • OperatorsName

Study/Series Identifiers:

  • StudyID
  • AccessionNumber
  • StudyDate, SeriesDate, AcquisitionDate, ContentDate
  • StudyTime, SeriesTime, AcquisitionTime, ContentTime

docs.title: UID Tags
docs.text:
These UIDs are regenerated by default:

  • StudyInstanceUID
  • SeriesInstanceUID
  • SOPInstanceUID
  • FrameOfReferenceUID

docs.title: What PACS Integration Does
docs.text:
• Query PACS server for studies
  • Retrieve DICOM files from PACS
  • Send anonymized files to PACS (optional)

docs.title: Configuration Steps
docs.text:
1. Obtain PACS credentials
  2. Contact your PACS administrator

  3. Get AE title, host, and port

  4. Configure environment bash DICOM_PACS_HOST=pacs.hospital.local DICOM_PACS_PORT=11112 DICOM_PACS_AE_TITLE=HALO_CORE

  5. Test connection

  6. Use DICOM Tools page to verify connectivity
  7. Check logs for connection errors

docs.title: Security Considerations
docs.text:
• PACS connections should be on secure networks
  • Consider VPN for remote access
  • Anonymize before sending to external PACS
  • Audit access to PACS integration

docs.title: HIPAA Safe Harbor
docs.text:
Default anonymization follows HIPAA Safe Harbor guidelines, removing all 18 identifiers:

  1. Names
  2. Geographic data
  3. Dates (except year)
  4. Phone numbers
  5. Fax numbers
  6. Email addresses
  7. Social Security numbers
  8. Medical record numbers
  9. Health plan beneficiary numbers
  10. Account numbers
  11. Certificate/license numbers
  12. Vehicle identifiers
  13. Device identifiers
  14. Web URLs
  15. IP addresses
  16. Biometric identifiers
  17. Full-face photos
  18. Any other unique identifying number

docs.title: GDPR Considerations
docs.text:
For EU data subjects:

  • Document anonymization process
  • Ensure irreversibility of anonymization
  • Consider pseudonymization if linkage is needed
  • Maintain records of processing activities

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
After anonymization:

  1. Verify no PHI remains in standard tags
  2. Check private tags are removed
  3. Confirm UIDs are new and unique
  4. Test file opens in DICOM viewer

docs.title: PACS Connection Fails
docs.text:
1. Verify network connectivity to PACS host
  2. Check firewall rules for PACS port
  3. Verify AE title is registered with PACS
  4. Check PACS server logs

docs.title: Auto-Anonymization Not Working
docs.text:
1. Verify DICOM_ANONYMIZE_ON_UPLOAD=true
  2. Check file is valid DICOM format
  3. Review application logs for errors
  4. Verify pydicom dependency is installed

docs.title: Anonymization Incomplete
docs.text:
1. Check if tags are in selection
  2. Verify private tag removal is enabled
  3. Check for nested sequences containing PHI
  4. Review anonymization mapping output

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
Required Python packages:

pip install pydicom

Optional for PACS:

pip install pynetdicom

docs.title: Maintenance
docs.text: Backup, updates, and migration procedures for HALO Core administrators.

docs.title: What to Back Up
docs.text: Data Location Frequency Sources data/sources/ Daily Chat history data/chat_history/ Daily Notes data/notes/ Daily Outputs data/outputs/ Daily Vector DB data/lancedb/ Daily Agent configs data/agents/ On change App config data/config.json On change

docs.title: Backup Script
docs.text:
#!/bin/bash
# backup.sh

DATE=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
BACKUP_DIR="/backups/halo/$DATE"
DATA_DIR="${HALO_DATA_DIR:-data}"

# Create backup directory
mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

# Backup data
cp -r "$DATA_DIR" "$BACKUP_DIR/data"

# Backup configuration
cp config.json "$BACKUP_DIR/" 2>/dev/null
cp presets.json "$BACKUP_DIR/" 2>/dev/null
cp templates/studio_templates.json "$BACKUP_DIR/" 2>/dev/null

# Compress
tar -czf "$BACKUP_DIR.tar.gz" -C "/backups/halo" "$DATE"
rm -rf "$BACKUP_DIR"

echo "Backup created: $BACKUP_DIR.tar.gz"

docs.title: Automated Backups
docs.text:
Cron job:

# Daily backup at 2 AM
0 2 * * * /opt/halo/scripts/backup.sh >> /var/log/halo/backup.log 2>&1

Systemd timer:

# /etc/systemd/system/halo-backup.timer
[Unit]
Description=HALO Core Daily Backup

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*-*-* 02:00:00
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

docs.title: Backup Retention
docs.text:
# Delete backups older than 30 days
find /backups/halo -name "*.tar.gz" -mtime +30 -delete

docs.title: Restore Procedure
docs.text:
# Extract backup
tar -xzf /backups/halo/20240115_020000.tar.gz

# Restore data
cp -r 20240115_020000/data/* ${HALO_DATA_DIR:-data}/

# Restore configuration
cp 20240115_020000/config.json ./
cp 20240115_020000/presets.json ./

docs.title: Update Process
docs.text:
1. Backup current state bash ./scripts/backup.sh

  2. Pull latest changes bash git pull origin main

  3. Update dependencies bash pip install -r requirements.txt --upgrade

#!/bin/bash

RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8501)

exit 0

docs.title: Enabling Memory Backend
docs.text:
Set HALO_AGENT_DB environment variable:

# SQLite (recommended for single instance)
export HALO_AGENT_DB="sqlite:///data/memory.db"

# PostgreSQL (for distributed setups)
export HALO_AGENT_DB="postgresql://user:pass@host/db"

docs.title: Memory Behavior
docs.text:

  • Agents use bounded history (num_history_runs=3)
  • User memories persist across sessions
  • Account page displays memory entries

  • Memory entries (if backend enabled)

docs.title: HALO Core User Handbook
docs.text: Welcome to the HALO Core User Handbook. This guide helps you get the most out of HALO Core — your workspace for research and content creation.

docs.title: What is HALO Core?
docs.text:
HALO (Holistic Agent Logical Orchestrator) Core is a workspace app that helps you:

  • Collect sources — files, web findings, connector results
  • Ask questions — source-grounded AI chat
  • Generate outputs — reports, infographics, podcasts, presentations
  • Save insights — reusable notes from your work

In short: HALO Core combines a document library, an AI assistant, and a content studio in one interface.

docs.title: Team-based AI Collaboration
docs.text: HALO can run with a team of specialized agents. Each agent has its own role, instructions, tools, and settings. This allows different expert perspectives — research-focused, report-focused, or tool-focused behavior.

docs.title: Smart Delegation Strategies
docs.text:
HALO supports multiple delegation modes:

Mode Behavior direct_only Master agent answers directly delegate_on_complexity Delegates to specialists by skills always_delegate Delegates to all configured members coordinated_rag Delegates broadly with source-first RAG guidance

docs.title: Transparency
docs.text:
Users can inspect what happened during generation via expandable sections:

  • Agent Actions
  • Tool Calls
  • Agent Thinking

This gives visibility into reasoning flow without exposing raw internals.

docs.title: Memory Management
docs.text: HALO supports a memory layer linked to the active user. When memory backend is enabled, user memory persists across interactions and can be managed in the Account page.

docs.title: Multimodal Understanding
docs.text:
Users can combine in one chat flow:

  • Text prompts
  • Image uploads
  • Audio input (with transcription)

docs.title: Source-Grounded Citations
docs.text: HALO answers using selected sources with citation-aware response handling and fallback resilience when streaming output is empty.

docs.title: Studio Pipeline
docs.text: HALO turns research context directly into production-style outputs (report, infographic, podcast, presentation, etc.). Outputs are persisted, revisitable, and exportable.

docs.title: Who HALO Core is For
docs.text:
HALO Core is designed for people who need to turn information into useful outputs quickly:

  • Analysts and consultants
  • Medical or scientific researchers
  • Content teams and editors
  • Product and strategy teams
  • Anyone preparing summaries, presentations, or evidence-based answers

docs.title: Key Benefits
docs.text:
• Faster research cycles — fewer app switches
  • More reliable answers — chat is grounded in selected sources
  • Reusable knowledge — notes and outputs persist locally
  • Flexible setup — configurable models, tools, presets, and sidebar layout
  • Practical output formats — markdown, PDF-like export, and slide-style CSV export

docs.text: Mode Behavior direct_only Master agent answers directly delegate_on_complexity Delegates based on skills match always_delegate Delegates to all members coordinated_rag Source-first RAG with delegation

docs.title: Stream Events
docs.text:
• stream_events: Enable event-rich streaming
  • log_stream_events: Verbose event logging

docs.title: Data Directory
docs.text:
Set HALO_DATA_DIR environment variable:

# Windows PowerShell
$env:HALO_DATA_DIR = "C:\data\halo"

# Linux/macOS
export HALO_DATA_DIR="/data/halo"

docs.title: Memory Backend
docs.text:
Set HALO_AGENT_DB for persistent memory:

$env:HALO_AGENT_DB = "sqlite:///data/memory.db"

When enabled:

  • Agents use bounded history (num_history_runs=3)
  • User memories persist across sessions
  • Account page shows memory entries

docs.title: Templates Directory
docs.text:
Set HALO_TEMPLATES_DIR for custom templates:

$env:HALO_TEMPLATES_DIR = "custom_templates"

docs.title: Member Configuration
docs.text:
In Agent Config, for each member:

  1. Set enabled state
  2. Define skills (for delegation matching)
  3. Configure tools
  4. Write role instructions

docs.title: Skills Matching
docs.text:
When using delegate_on_complexity:

  • Agent skills are matched against prompt
  • Matching members receive delegation
  • Non-matching members are skipped

docs.title: Example Skills
docs.text:
{
  "skills": ["research", "analysis", "medical", "writing"]
}

docs.title: Knowledge Base
docs.text:
• LanceDB stored in data/lancedb
  • Automatic indexing of sources
  • Vector search for retrieval

docs.title: Search Types
docs.text:
Type Description Hybrid Vector + full-text search Vector Vector similarity only

!!! note On Windows, vector search is used to avoid hybrid/FTS index lock issues.

docs.title: "Team members not active"
docs.text:
Check:

  1. Member enabled is true
  2. Coordination mode allows delegation
  3. Skills match the prompt

docs.title: "Knowledge retrieval fails"
docs.text:
Check:

  1. LanceDB directory exists
  2. Sources are indexed
  3. Check logs for errors

docs.title: "Custom settings don't apply"
docs.text:
Try:

  1. Save configuration
  2. Restart the app
  3. Verify environment variables

docs.title: Chat
docs.text: The Chat panel is where you interact with the AI assistant using your selected sources.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
• Ask text questions grounded in your sources
  • Attach images for multimodal analysis
  • Record or upload audio for transcription
  • View tool calls and agent actions
  • Save responses as notes

docs.title: Why It Matters
docs.text: Chat is the primary interface for turning your source content into insights. Grounded responses are more trustworthy because they cite your actual documents.

docs.title: Input Area
docs.text:
• Text input: Type your question or prompt
  • Image upload: Attach images for analysis
  • Audio input: Record or upload audio for transcription

docs.title: Response Area
docs.text:
• Main response: AI-generated answer
  • Source section: Citations from selected sources
  • Expandable sections: Agent Actions, Tool Calls, Agent Thinking

docs.title: Basic Flow
docs.text:
1. Select sources in the Sources panel
  2. In Chat, enter your prompt
  3. Press Enter or click Send
  4. Review the response

docs.title: Expected Results
docs.text:
• Response appears in the chat area
  • Source citations are included when applicable
  • Tool calls show if tools were used

docs.title: Images
docs.text:
1. Click the image icon in the input area
  2. Upload an image file
  3. Enter a prompt about the image
  4. Send

Use cases:

  • Analyze charts or diagrams
  • Extract text from screenshots
  • Describe visual content

docs.title: Audio
docs.text:
1. Click the audio icon in the input area
  2. Record audio or upload an audio file
  3. Audio is transcribed to text
  4. Edit the transcribed prompt if needed
  5. Send

Use cases:

  • Voice notes as prompts
  • Transcribe meetings or lectures
  • Hands-free input

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text: Creates a unified summary of all sources in your library.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
1. In the chat panel, open Zusammenfassung aller Quellen
  2. Click the refresh icon to generate/update
  3. Optionally pin the summary to notes

docs.title: Expected Results
docs.text:
• Summary content is displayed
  • Stale indicator appears when source set has changed
  • You can save summary as a note for later reuse

docs.title: Source Citations
docs.text:
When sources are selected, responses may include:

  • Source names referenced
  • Relevant excerpts quoted
  • Links to source documents

docs.title: Agent Actions
docs.text:
Expand this section to see:

  • Which agent(s) were involved
  • Delegation decisions
  • Reasoning flow

docs.title: Tool Calls
docs.text:
Expand this section to see:

  • Tools used (e.g., web search, calculator)
  • Input parameters
  • Results returned

docs.title: Agent Thinking
docs.text:
Expand this section to see:

  • Internal reasoning steps
  • Decision points
  • Confidence indicators

docs.title: Save as Note
docs.text:
1. Below a response, click In Notiz speichern
  2. Note is saved to the Notes section
  3. Note can be reused as a source

docs.title: Why Save
docs.text:
• Preserve important findings
  • Build a knowledge base
  • Reuse content in future queries

docs.title: Chat History
docs.text:
• Previous conversations are stored in data/chat_history/
  • History persists across sessions
  • Each session maintains its own context

docs.title: "I get weak or generic answers"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • No sources selected
  • Source summary outdated
  • Missing API key

Try this:

  1. Confirm selected sources in Sources panel
  2. Refresh all-sources summary
  3. Verify OPENAI_API_KEY is configured

docs.title: "Chat streaming fails"
docs.text:
HALO has fallback behavior to still generate responses when possible.

Try this:

  1. Check error messages in the UI
  2. Verify API connectivity
  3. Try a simpler prompt

docs.title: "Audio transcription fails"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Missing FFmpeg
  • Unsupported audio format
  • API key issue

Try this:

  1. Verify FFmpeg is in PATH
  2. Try a different audio format
  3. Check OpenAI API key

docs.title: DICOM Tools
docs.text: DICOM Tools provides medical imaging anonymization for privacy-compliant handling of DICOM files.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
• Anonymize DICOM files — Remove Protected Health Information (PHI)
  • Regenerate UIDs — Create new unique identifiers for studies, series, and instances
  • Remove private tags — Strip manufacturer-specific metadata
  • Export mapping — Save anonymization mapping as CSV for reference

docs.title: Why It Matters
docs.text:
Medical imaging files (DICOM) contain sensitive patient information. Sharing these files for:

  • Research collaboration
  • Teaching and presentations
  • Second opinions
  • Publications

...requires removing identifiable information to comply with HIPAA, GDPR, and other privacy regulations.

docs.title: Accessing DICOM Tools
docs.text:
1. Open HALO Core
  2. Navigate to DICOM Tools in the sidebar
  3. The page displays anonymization options

docs.title: What Gets Anonymized
docs.text:
By default, HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers are removed:

Category Tags Anonymized Patient Data Name, ID, Birth Date, Sex, Age, Address, Weight, Phone, Other IDs Institution Data Name, Address, Referring Physician Physician Data Performing Physician, Operators Study Data Study ID, Accession Number, Dates, Times Series Data Series Date/Time, Acquisition Date/Time

docs.title: UID Regeneration
docs.text:
When enabled (default), new UIDs are generated for:

  • Study Instance UID
  • Series Instance UID
  • SOP Instance UID
  • Frame of Reference UID

This ensures anonymized files don't link back to original studies.

docs.title: Private Tags
docs.text: Private tags are manufacturer-specific and may contain identifying information. They are removed by default.

docs.title: Step 1: Upload Files
docs.text:
1. Click Dateien hochladen
  2. Select DICOM files (.dcm, .dicom, or files without extension)
  3. Multiple files can be uploaded at once

docs.title: Step 2: Configure Options
docs.text:
Select what to anonymize:

  • UIDs neu generieren — Regenerate unique identifiers (recommended)
  • Private Tags entfernen — Remove manufacturer-specific tags (recommended)
  • Patientendaten — Patient name, ID, birth date, etc.
  • Institutionsdaten — Hospital/clinic information
  • Studiendaten — Study ID, accession number
  • Datumsangaben — All date/time fields

docs.title: Step 3: Anonymize
docs.text:
1. Click Anonymisieren
  2. Wait for processing to complete
  3. Review results in the output section

docs.title: Step 4: Download
docs.text:
1. Download anonymized files as ZIP
  2. Optionally download the anonymization mapping CSV

docs.title: Expected Results
docs.text:
After anonymization:

  • All selected tags are cleared or replaced with placeholder values
  • New UIDs are generated (if enabled)
  • Private tags are removed (if enabled)
  • Files are functionally identical for clinical review
  • Files can be shared without exposing patient identity

docs.title: Anonymization Mapping
docs.text:
The mapping CSV contains:

Column Description Original Filename Original file name Anonymized Filename New file name Original Patient ID Original patient identifier Anonymized ID New anonymized identifier Tags Anonymized List of cleared tags UIDs Regenerated List of regenerated UIDs

Keep this mapping secure — it links anonymized files to original patients.

docs.title: Auto-Anonymization on Upload
docs.text: When enabled in configuration, DICOM files uploaded to Sources are automatically anonymized.

docs.title: Enable in .env:
docs.text: DICOM_ANONYMIZE_ON_UPLOAD=true

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Files are anonymized before being added to Sources
  • Original files are not stored
  • Mapping is saved for reference

docs.title: "File not recognized as DICOM"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • File is not a valid DICOM file
  • File is corrupted
  • Unsupported DICOM format

Try this:

  1. Verify file is a valid DICOM file
  2. Try opening in a DICOM viewer first
  3. Check file integrity

docs.title: "Anonymization fails"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • File is write-protected
  • Missing dependencies
  • Invalid DICOM structure

Try this:

  1. Check file permissions
  2. Verify pydicom is installed
  3. Try a different DICOM file

docs.title: "Download fails"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Browser blocking download
  • Large file size
  • Disk space issue

Try this:

  1. Enable pop-ups/downloads for the site
  2. Process fewer files at once
  3. Check available disk space

docs.title: Before Anonymizing
docs.text:
• Verify files are correct DICOM format
  • Note any critical metadata that may be needed later
  • Consider which tags are necessary for your use case

docs.title: After Anonymizing
docs.text:
• Verify anonymized files open correctly
  • Check that clinical utility is preserved
  • Securely store mapping file if patient linkage is needed
  • Delete original files if no longer needed

docs.title: For Research
docs.text:
• Document anonymization process for IRB compliance
  • Store mapping separately from anonymized files
  • Consider de-identification beyond HIPAA Safe Harbor if needed

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Sources — Upload anonymized files to your library
  • DICOM Configuration — Admin setup

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text: Quick answers to common questions about HALO Core.

docs.title: Q: Do I need an account to use HALO Core?
docs.text: No mandatory signup is required in the default local setup. The app uses a local user identity (local-user) by default.

docs.title: Q: Where is my data stored?
docs.text: By default, data is stored in local JSON files under the data/ directory. You can change this with the HALO_DATA_DIR environment variable.

docs.title: Q: Is HALO Core free to use?
docs.text: The application is open source. However, AI features require API keys (e.g., OpenAI) which have their own pricing.

docs.title: Q: Can I use HALO Core offline?
docs.text: Basic functionality works offline, but AI chat features require API connectivity to your configured model provider.

docs.title: Q: Can I use only one source?
docs.text: Yes. You can select one or many sources per task. Select what's relevant for your current query.

docs.title: Q: What file types are supported?
docs.text: Documents (PDF, DOCX, TXT, MD), spreadsheets (CSV, XLSX), presentations (PPTX), images (PNG, JPG, etc.), audio (MP3, WAV, etc.), and video (MP4, MOV, etc.).

docs.title: Q: How do I remove a source?
docs.text: Click the source menu (⋮) and select Delete. Confirm the deletion.

docs.title: Q: Can I change the AI model?
docs.text: Yes. Use Configuration (Chat tab) to select a different model, or use Agent Config for detailed control.

docs.title: Q: Why are my answers generic?
docs.text: Likely no sources are selected, or the source summary is outdated. Select relevant sources and refresh the summary.

docs.title: Q: Can I use images and audio in chat?
docs.text: Yes. Chat input supports image uploads and audio capture/import for multimodal queries.

docs.title: Q: What if chat streaming fails?
docs.text: The runtime has fallback behavior to still generate responses when possible. Check error messages and retry.

docs.title: Q: What outputs can I generate?
docs.text: Reports, infographics, podcast scripts, video scripts, presentations, and data tables.

docs.title: Q: Can I export generated outputs?
docs.text: Yes. Outputs can be downloaded from the Studio-Ergebnisse section.

docs.title: Q: Why is my output quality poor?
docs.text: Ensure you have relevant sources selected, provide specific extra prompts, and check your tone settings.

docs.title: Q: How do I reset menu/sidebar customization?
docs.text: Use Configuration → App and reset sidebar menu settings, or manually edit the configuration.

docs.title: Q: Where are presets stored?
docs.text: Presets are stored in presets.json in the project root.

docs.title: Q: Can I have multiple configurations?
docs.text: You can use different HALO_DATA_DIR values for different environments (dev, staging, prod).

docs.title: Q: Can I control which tools an agent uses?
docs.text: Yes. Use Agent Config to enable/disable specific tools per agent.

docs.title: Q: What are coordination modes?
docs.text:
Rules for how master and team agents collaborate:

Mode Behavior direct_only Master answers directly delegate_on_complexity Delegates by skill match always_delegate Delegates to all members coordinated_rag Source-first RAG with delegation

docs.title: Q: Why aren't team members responding?
docs.text: Check that members are enabled, coordination mode allows delegation, and your prompt matches member skills.

docs.title: Q: How do I enable persistent memory?
docs.text: Set the HALO_AGENT_DB environment variable to a database path.

docs.title: Q: Where is memory stored?
docs.text: Memory is stored in the database specified by HALO_AGENT_DB, or in JSON files if not configured.

docs.title: Q: How do I clear my memory?
docs.text: Use the Account page to view and delete memory entries.

docs.title: Q: What Python version do I need?
docs.text: Python 3.10 or higher. Python 3.11 is recommended.

docs.title: Q: What dependencies are required?
docs.text: See requirements.txt for the full list. Key dependencies include Streamlit, Agno, OpenAI SDK, and various parsing libraries.

docs.title: Q: How do I run tests?
docs.text:
pytest

Or for specific tests:

pytest tests/test_chat_runtime.py -v

docs.title: Q: How do I run the docs locally?
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: Q: Can I deploy HALO Core to the cloud?
docs.text:
Yes. Options include:

  • Streamlit Cloud (demo hosting)
  • Self-hosted Docker containers
  • Cloud VMs with manual setup

docs.title: Q: Is every connector production-ready?
docs.text: Not necessarily in this repository snapshot. Some connector flows are MVP/mock-oriented and may need extension for production use.

docs.title: Q: How do I secure my API keys?
docs.text: Store keys in .streamlit/secrets.toml or environment variables. Never commit secrets to version control.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide walks you through installing, configuring, and using HALO Core for the first time.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before running HALO Core, make sure you have:

  • Python 3.10+ (3.11 recommended)
  • FFmpeg — required for audio/video transcription
  • OpenAI API key — for full AI features
  • Optional: Node/npm for frontend tooling

docs.title: 1. Create Virtual Environment
docs.text:
python -m venv .venv
. .venv/Scripts/activate        # Windows PowerShell
# or
source .venv/bin/activate       # Linux/macOS

docs.title: 2. Install Dependencies
docs.text:
pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: 3. Configure Secrets
docs.text:
Create .streamlit/secrets.toml:

OPENAI_API_KEY = "sk-..."

Additional optional keys:

# For memory backend
HALO_AGENT_DB = "sqlite:///data/memory.db"

# For custom data directory
HALO_DATA_DIR = "data"

docs.title: Running the App
docs.text:
streamlit run app/main.py

The app opens in your browser at http://localhost:8501.

docs.title: First-Use Flow
docs.text: Follow these steps for your first successful workflow:

docs.title: Step 1: Add Sources
docs.text:
1. Open Sources panel (left column)
  2. Click + Quellen hinzufügen
  3. Upload one or more files (PDF, DOCX, TXT, MD, CSV, XLSX, PPTX, images, audio, video)
  4. Wait for ingestion to complete

docs.title: Step 2: Select Sources
docs.text:
1. Mark sources via checkboxes
  2. Or use "select all" for bulk selection

docs.title: Step 3: Ask a Question
docs.text:
1. Open Chat panel (center column)
  2. Enter a prompt in the text input
  3. Optionally attach an image or record audio
  4. Press Enter or click Send

docs.title: Step 4: Review the Answer
docs.text:
• The response appears in the chat area
  • Expand Agent Actions or Tool Calls for details
  • Check source citations if applicable

docs.title: Step 5: Save as Note
docs.text:
1. Click In Notiz speichern below the response
  2. The note appears in the Notes section

docs.title: Step 6: Generate Output
docs.text:
1. Open Studio panel (right column)
  2. Choose a template (e.g., Bericht)
  3. Click the generate button
  4. Review the output in Studio-Ergebnisse

docs.title: Expected Result
docs.text:
You now have a complete source-to-output workflow:

  1. ✅ Sources imported and selected
  2. ✅ Question asked and answered
  3. ✅ Answer saved as note
  4. ✅ Structured output generated

docs.title: Account and Identity
docs.text:
There is no mandatory account sign-up in the UI:

  • Default user identity is local-user
  • Advanced users can set a custom user_id in configuration
  • Memory backend enables persistent user memory across sessions

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Definitions of terms used throughout HALO Core documentation.

docs.title: Agent
docs.text: An AI role with its own instructions, tools, and settings. Agents can work independently or as part of a team.

docs.title: Agent Config
docs.text: The configuration page for advanced agent-level settings including tools, MCP servers, and coordination mode.

docs.title: Chat History
docs.text: Stored conversation records, persisted in data/chat_history/.

docs.title: Connector
docs.text: An integration path to external systems for source discovery. Examples include Drive and Notion connectors.

docs.title: Coordination Mode
docs.text: A rule for how master and team agents collaborate. Options: direct_only, delegate_on_complexity, always_delegate, coordinated_rag.

docs.title: Data Directory
docs.text: The folder where HALO stores all persistent data. Default: data/. Configurable via HALO_DATA_DIR.

docs.title: Fallback
docs.text: Alternative generation path when primary streaming fails. HALO has built-in fallback behavior.

docs.title: Grounded Answer
docs.text: A response tied to selected sources rather than pure free-form output. Grounded answers cite actual source content.

docs.title: HALO
docs.text: Holistic Agent Logical Orchestrator — the full name of the HALO Core system.

docs.title: Knowledge
docs.text: The indexed content from sources, stored in LanceDB for retrieval. Enables RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation).

docs.title: LanceDB
docs.text: A vector database used by HALO for knowledge storage and similarity search.

docs.title: Master Agent
docs.text: The primary agent that receives user queries and optionally delegates to team members.

docs.title: MCP Server
docs.text: Model Context Protocol server — an external capability endpoint that provides additional tools to agents.

docs.title: Memory Backend
docs.text: An optional database layer for persistent user/agent memory. Enabled via HALO_AGENT_DB.

docs.title: Multimodal
docs.text: Input that combines multiple types: text, images, and audio.

docs.title: Note
docs.text: A saved text snippet from chat, summary, or manual input. Notes can be converted to sources.

docs.title: Preset
docs.text: A saved chat setup bundle containing model, tools, and team member configuration.

docs.title: RAG
docs.text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation — using retrieved knowledge to ground AI responses in source content.

docs.title: Session
docs.text: A single continuous interaction with HALO. Sessions maintain context and history.

docs.title: Source
docs.text: Any imported content item used as knowledge input for chat and studio.

docs.title: Source Summary
docs.text: A generated overview of all sources in the library, available in the Chat panel.

docs.title: Stream Events
docs.text: Real-time internal events shown during response generation. Controlled by stream_events setting.

docs.title: Studio
docs.text: The panel for template-driven output generation (reports, presentations, etc.).

docs.title: Studio Output
docs.text: A generated artifact saved in Studio-Ergebnisse.

docs.title: Team Agent
docs.text: A specialized agent that works alongside the master agent. Team members have specific roles and skills.

docs.title: Template
docs.text: A predefined studio output type (report, infographic, podcast, presentation, data table).

docs.title: Tool
docs.text: A capability available to agents, such as web search, calculator, or file operations.

docs.title: Trace
docs.text: Structured information about agent actions, tool calls, and reasoning during generation.

docs.title: Vector Search
docs.text: Similarity-based search using vector embeddings. Used for knowledge retrieval.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text: A structured sequence of steps to accomplish a specific task in HALO.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Purpose OPENAI_API_KEY OpenAI API authentication HALO_DATA_DIR Custom data directory path HALO_TEMPLATES_DIR Custom templates directory HALO_AGENT_DB Memory backend database path

docs.title: File Types
docs.text: Extension Category .pdf, .docx, .txt, .md Documents .csv, .xlsx, .xls Spreadsheets .pptx Presentations .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .webp Images .mp3, .wav, .m4a, .ogg Audio .mp4, .mov, .avi, .mkv Video

docs.title: Configuration Files
docs.text: File Content config.json App settings presets.json Chat presets data/agents/*.json Agent configurations templates/studio_templates.json Studio templates .streamlit/secrets.toml API keys and secrets

docs.title: Notes Management
docs.text: Notes store reusable content captured from chat, summaries, or manual entry.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
• Save chat responses as reusable notes
  • Create manual notes from scratch
  • Convert notes into sources for future queries
  • Organize, rename, download, and delete notes

docs.title: Why It Matters
docs.text: Notes preserve key findings and allow reusing them as sources. This builds a personal knowledge base within HALO Core.

docs.title: From Chat Response
docs.text:
1. Below a chat response, click In Notiz speichern
  2. Note is saved with the response content
  3. Source info is preserved

docs.title: From Source Summary
docs.text:
1. In the summary section, click save
  2. Summary becomes a note
  3. Can be used as a source

docs.title: Manual Note
docs.text:
1. In notes section, click Notiz hinzufügen
  2. Enter title and content
  3. Save

docs.title: Expand a Note
docs.text:
1. Click on a note to expand
  2. View content and source info
  3. See creation timestamp

docs.title: Note Details
docs.text: Field Description Title Note name Content Saved text Source Origin (chat, summary, manual) Date Creation timestamp

docs.title: Rename
docs.text:
1. Click the note menu (⋮)
  2. Select Rename
  3. Enter new name
  4. Confirm

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
1. Click the note menu (⋮)
  2. Select Download
  3. File downloads to your computer

docs.title: Convert to Source
docs.text:
1. Click the note menu (⋮)
  2. Select Convert to Source
  3. Note appears in Sources list
  4. Can be selected for chat/studio

docs.title: Delete
docs.text:
1. Click the note menu (⋮)
  2. Select Delete
  3. Confirm deletion

!!! warning Deleting a note removes it permanently. This cannot be undone.

docs.title: Notes vs Sources
docs.text: Aspect Notes Sources Origin Created in HALO Imported/uploaded Purpose Save insights Input for AI Conversion Can become sources Cannot become notes Editing Can be edited Cannot be edited

docs.title: What to Save as Notes
docs.text:
• Key findings from chat
  • Important summaries
  • Decisions and conclusions
  • Reusable explanations

docs.title: What Not to Save
docs.text:
• Temporary queries
  • Incomplete responses
  • Content you'll delete soon

docs.title: Organization
docs.text:
• Use descriptive titles
  • Convert important notes to sources
  • Delete outdated notes regularly

docs.title: "Note doesn't appear after saving"
docs.text:
Try this:

  1. Refresh the notes section
  2. Check for error messages
  3. Verify data directory is writable

docs.title: "Convert to source fails"
docs.text:
Try this:

  1. Ensure note has content
  2. Check for special characters in title
  3. Verify source list refreshes

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Sources — Manage converted notes
  • Chat — Create more notes from responses
  • Workflows — Note-based workflows

docs.title: Sources Library
docs.text: The Sources library is where you import, manage, select, and remove content used by chat and studio generation.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
• Import files from your computer
  • Fetch content from connected systems (connectors)
  • Select which sources the AI can use
  • Organize, rename, download, and delete sources

docs.title: Why It Matters
docs.text: Source selection controls what the AI can use for grounded responses. Without selected sources, HALO answers from general knowledge rather than your specific content.

docs.title: Supported File Types
docs.text: Category Extensions Documents .pdf, .docx, .txt, .md Spreadsheets .csv, .xlsx, .xls Presentations .pptx Images .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .webp Audio .mp3, .wav, .m4a, .ogg Video .mp4, .mov, .avi, .mkv Medical Imaging .dcm, .dicom

docs.title: Upload Files
docs.text:
1. In Sources, click + Quellen hinzufügen
  2. Select one or more files from your computer
  3. Wait for upload and ingestion to complete
  4. Imported items appear in the source list

docs.title: Expected Results
docs.text:
• Source appears with type icon and timestamp
  • Content is parsed and indexed for search
  • Source is available for selection

docs.title: Connector-Based Collection
docs.text: Connectors fetch source suggestions from external systems.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text:
1. In Sources, choose one or more connectors
  2. Click Quellen abrufen
  3. Review listed results
  4. Click Importieren for items you want

docs.title: Expected Results
docs.text:
• Imported connector items appear in your source list
  • They can be selected like uploaded files

!!! note Some connector behavior is MVP/mock-backed in this version. Treat it as a guided ingestion layer that can be extended in production.

docs.title: Individual Selection
docs.text:
1. Click the checkbox next to each source
  2. Selected sources are highlighted

docs.title: Bulk Selection
docs.text:
1. Use "select all" checkbox
  2. Or use the dropdown menu for bulk actions

docs.title: What Selection Affects
docs.text:
• Chat queries use selected sources only
  • Studio generation uses selected sources for context
  • Source summary reflects selected sources

docs.title: Rename
docs.text:
1. Click the source menu (⋮)
  2. Select Rename
  3. Enter new name
  4. Confirm

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
1. Click the source menu (⋮)
  2. Select Download
  3. File downloads to your computer

docs.title: Delete
docs.text:
1. Click the source menu (⋮)
  2. Select Delete
  3. Confirm deletion

!!! warning Deleting a source removes it from local project state. This cannot be undone.

docs.title: Source List View
docs.text:
The source list shows:

Column Description Checkbox Selection state Icon File type indicator Name Source filename/title Type File extension Date Import timestamp Menu Actions dropdown

docs.title: "My file fails to import"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Unsupported file extension
  • Corrupted file
  • Missing parsing dependency

Try this:

  1. Check supported file types above
  2. For audio/video, verify FFmpeg is installed
  3. Try a smaller or simpler file

docs.title: "Connector returns no results"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Connector not configured
  • No matching content in connected system

Try this:

  1. Verify connector credentials in configuration
  2. Check connector-specific settings

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Chat — Use selected sources in conversations
  • Studio — Generate outputs from source context
  • Notes — Convert notes into sources
  • DICOM Tools — Anonymize medical imaging files

docs.title: Studio
docs.text: The Studio panel is where you generate structured deliverables from your current context using template cards.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
• Generate reports, infographics, podcasts, presentations, and data tables
  • Customize output language, tone, and focus
  • Store and export generated artifacts
  • Link outputs to source context

docs.title: Why It Matters
docs.text: Studio transforms chat/research context into actionable artifacts quickly. Instead of manually formatting outputs, you use templates designed for specific deliverable types.

docs.title: Available Templates
docs.text: Template Description Bericht (Report) Structured written report Infografik Visual infographic outline Podcast Podcast script and structure Videoubersicht Video script and storyboard Präsentation Slide deck outline Datentabelle Structured data table

docs.title: Basic Flow
docs.text:
1. Open Studio panel (right column)
  2. Pick a template card
  3. Click the generate button
  4. Review saved output in Studio-Ergebnisse

docs.title: Customizing Output
docs.text:
Before generating, you can customize:

  1. Click the card menu (⋮)
  2. Set options:
  3. Language: Output language
  4. Tone: Writing style
  5. Extra prompt: Additional instructions
  6. Generate

docs.title: Expected Results
docs.text:
After generation:

  • New output artifact appears in Studio results
  • Output includes generation timestamp
  • Source context is linked
  • You can expand, review, export, or delete

docs.title: View Output
docs.text:
1. In Studio-Ergebnisse, find the output
  2. Click to expand and read content

docs.title: Export Output
docs.text:
1. Click the output menu (⋮)
  2. Select Download
  3. Choose format (if applicable)

docs.title: Delete Output
docs.text:
1. Click the output menu (⋮)
  2. Select Delete
  3. Confirm deletion

docs.title: Output Details
docs.text:
Each output includes:

Field Description Title Generated or custom title Type Template used Timestamp When generated Sources Linked source context Content Generated text

docs.title: Before Generation
docs.text:
• Ensure relevant sources are selected
  • Review chat context for key findings
  • Set appropriate language and tone

docs.title: After Generation
docs.text:
• Review output for accuracy
  • Edit or refine as needed
  • Export for external use

docs.title: For Reports
docs.text:
• Use Bericht template for comprehensive documents
  • Include extra prompt for specific focus areas

docs.title: For Presentations
docs.text:
• Use Präsentation template for slide outlines
  • Export and refine in presentation software

docs.title: "No studio outputs generated"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • No relevant selected source context
  • Model/tool configuration issue
  • API connectivity problem

Try this:

  1. Confirm source selection
  2. Test with Bericht template first
  3. Check chat and agent configuration

docs.title: "Output quality is poor"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Insufficient source context
  • Vague extra prompt
  • Wrong tone setting

Try this:

  1. Add more relevant sources
  2. Provide specific extra prompt instructions
  3. Adjust tone setting

docs.text:

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: This guide helps you solve common problems in HALO Core.

docs.title: Problem: "I get weak or generic answers"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • No sources selected
  • Source summary outdated
  • Missing API key

Try this:

  1. Confirm selected sources in Sources panel
  2. Refresh all-sources summary
  3. Verify OPENAI_API_KEY is configured in .streamlit/secrets.toml
  4. Restart the app

docs.title: Problem: "Chat streaming fails"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • API connectivity issue
  • Model rate limit reached
  • Temporary service issue

Try this:

  1. Check error messages in the UI
  2. Wait a moment and retry
  3. Try a simpler prompt
  4. Verify API key is valid

docs.title: Problem: "Responses don't cite sources"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Sources not properly indexed
  • Query doesn't match source content
  • Model not using retrieval

Try this:

  1. Verify sources are selected
  2. Check source summary for content
  3. Make query more specific
  4. Enable knowledge retrieval in agent config

docs.title: Problem: "My uploaded file fails to import"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Unsupported file extension
  • Corrupted file
  • Missing parsing dependency

Try this:

  1. Check supported file types in Sources
  2. Try a smaller or simpler file
  3. For audio/video, verify FFmpeg is installed
  4. Check error message for details

docs.title: Problem: "Connector returns no results"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Connector not configured
  • No matching content
  • Authentication issue

Try this:

  1. Verify connector credentials in Configuration
  2. Check connector-specific settings
  3. Test with different search terms

docs.title: Problem: "Source list is empty after upload"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Upload didn't complete
  • Ingestion failed
  • Data directory issue

Try this:

  1. Check for error messages
  2. Verify data directory is writable
  3. Refresh the page
  4. Check disk space

docs.title: Problem: "Account page says memory backend disabled"
docs.text:
Cause: Optional memory DB is not configured.

Try this:

  1. Set HALO_AGENT_DB in environment:

```bash # Windows PowerShell $env:HALO_AGENT_DB = "sqlite:///data/memory.db"

# Linux/macOS export HALO_AGENT_DB="sqlite:///data/memory.db" ```

  1. Restart the app

docs.title: Problem: "Memory entries don't persist"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Memory backend not properly configured
  • Database file not writable
  • Session issue

Try this:

  1. Verify HALO_AGENT_DB path exists
  2. Check file permissions
  3. Restart the app

docs.title: Problem: "Preset list is empty"
docs.text:
Cause: presets.json missing or invalid.

Try this:

  1. Create presets.json in project root:

json { "presets": [ { "name": "Default", "model": "gpt-4o-mini", "tools": [], "members": [] } ] }

  1. Reopen Configuration → Chat

docs.title: Problem: "Configuration doesn't save"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • File permissions issue
  • Invalid value format
  • Disk space issue

Try this:

  1. Check file permissions on data/ directory
  2. Verify value formats are correct
  3. Ensure disk space is available
  4. Check for error messages

docs.title: Problem: "Changes don't take effect"
docs.text:
Try this:

  1. Save the configuration explicitly
  2. Refresh the app (press R)
  3. Restart the app if necessary

docs.title: Problem: "No studio outputs generated"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • No relevant source context
  • Model/tool configuration issue
  • API connectivity problem

Try this:

  1. Confirm source selection
  2. Test with Bericht template first
  3. Check chat configuration
  4. Verify API key

docs.title: Problem: "Output quality is poor"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Insufficient source context
  • Vague extra prompt
  • Wrong tone setting

Try this:

  1. Add more relevant sources
  2. Provide specific extra prompt instructions
  3. Adjust tone setting
  4. Try a different model

docs.title: Problem: "Team members not active"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Members disabled in agent config
  • Coordination mode set to direct_only
  • Prompt didn't match skills for delegation

Try this:

  1. Open Agent Config page
  2. Verify member enabled is true
  3. Check coordination_mode setting
  4. Inspect assistant trace in "Agent Actions"

docs.title: Problem: "Tool calls not displayed"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • stream_events disabled
  • Tools not configured for agent
  • Display setting issue

Try this:

  1. Verify stream_events: true in agent config
  2. Verify tool IDs are configured
  3. Enable stream debug logging

docs.title: Problem: "Knowledge retrieval fails"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • LanceDB not initialized
  • Sources not indexed
  • Windows FTS lock issue

Try this:

  1. Check data/lancedb directory exists
  2. Verify sources are ingested
  3. On Windows, vector search fallback should activate automatically
  4. Check logs for errors

docs.title: Problem: "Audio transcription fails"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Missing FFmpeg
  • Unsupported audio format
  • API key issue

Try this:

  1. Verify FFmpeg is in system PATH
  2. Try a different audio format (MP3, WAV)
  3. Check OpenAI API key

docs.title: Problem: "Video parsing issues"
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • Missing FFmpeg
  • Large file size
  • Unsupported format

Try this:

  1. Verify FFmpeg installation
  2. Try a smaller video file
  3. Check supported formats

docs.title: Problem: "LanceDB WinError 5 (_indices/fts)"
docs.text:
Cause: Windows file locking on FTS index.

Try this:

  1. Restart Streamlit
  2. Move HALO_DATA_DIR outside heavily locked sync folders
  3. Vector search fallback should activate automatically

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
If problems persist:

  1. Check logs for error details
  2. Review FAQ for common questions
  3. Consult Admin Guide for system issues
  4. Check GitHub issues for known problems

docs.title: Workflows
docs.text: This section provides step-by-step guides for common tasks.

docs.title: Workflow 1: Ask Grounded Questions on Uploaded Documents
Goal
docs.text: Get traceable answers tied to your source material.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Upload documents
  2. In Sources, click + Quellen hinzufügen
  3. Select PDFs, DOCX, or other files

  4. Wait for ingestion

  5. Select sources

  6. Mark uploaded files with checkboxes

  7. Ensure at least one source is selected

  8. Ask a focused question

  9. In Chat, enter your question

  10. Be specific about what you need

  11. Review the answer

  12. Check source citations

  13. Expand Agent Actions if needed

  14. Save the best answer

  15. Click In Notiz speichern
  16. Note appears in Notes section

docs.title: Result
docs.text: You have a traceable answer tied to selected material, saved for future reference.

docs.title: Workflow 2: Build a Report Draft from Collected Evidence
Goal
docs.text: Create a structured report from multiple sources.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Import sources
  2. Upload relevant files
  3. Use connectors for external content

  4. Select all relevant sources

  5. Generate summary

  6. In Chat, open Zusammenfassung aller Quellen
  7. Click refresh to generate

  8. Review the summary

  9. Open Studio

  10. Navigate to Studio panel

  11. Select Bericht template

  12. Customize output

  13. Open template menu
  14. Set language and tone

  15. Add extra prompt with focus areas

  16. Generate and export

  17. Click generate button
  18. Review output in Studio-Ergebnisse
  19. Download for external editing

docs.title: Result
docs.text: Report-style draft you can refine in external tools.

docs.title: Workflow 3: Convert Insights into Presentation-Ready Structure
Goal
docs.text: Create a slide deck outline from your research.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Prepare source set
  2. Select relevant documents

  3. Ensure key findings are captured

  4. Chat for key points

  5. Ask for main themes
  6. Request bullet points

  7. Save important responses as notes

  8. Use Präsentation template

  9. Open Studio
  10. Select Präsentation template

  11. Set language and style

  12. Generate output

  13. Click generate

  14. Review slide outline

  15. Export and refine

  16. Download the output
  17. Transfer to presentation software
  18. Add visuals and formatting

docs.title: Result
docs.text: Rapid first draft of slide flow ready for refinement.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Keep assistant behavior clean and relevant over time.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Review memory entries
  2. Open Account page

  3. View stored memory items

  4. Delete outdated entries

  5. Select irrelevant memories
  6. Click delete selected

  7. Or clear all if needed

  8. Keep useful context

  9. Leave entries that help
  10. Remove noise and outdated info

docs.title: Result
docs.text: Cleaner assistant behavior and reduced noise in responses.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Combine text, images, and audio in one analysis.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Upload image
  2. In Chat, click image icon

  3. Select image file

  4. Add audio context

  5. Click audio icon
  6. Record or upload audio

  7. Review transcription

  8. Enter prompt

  9. Ask about both image and audio content

  10. Request specific analysis

  11. Review response

  12. Check image analysis

  13. Verify audio context integration

  14. Save result

  15. Save as note for reference

docs.title: Result
docs.text: Comprehensive analysis combining multiple input types.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Rapidly synthesize information from multiple sources.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Select diverse sources
  2. Choose documents from different perspectives

  3. Include primary and secondary sources

  4. Generate all-sources summary

  5. Use summary feature

  6. Review key themes

  7. Ask synthesis question

  8. Request comparison or synthesis

  9. Ask for conflicting viewpoints

  10. Generate infographic

  11. Use Infografik template
  12. Visualize key findings

docs.title: Result
docs.text: Quick synthesis with visual output for sharing.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Create a podcast script from research material.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Prepare content
  2. Select source documents

  3. Generate summary for context

  4. Open Studio

  5. Select Podcast template

  6. Set language and tone

  7. Customize for your format

  8. Add extra prompt with:

    • Episode length
    • Target audience
    • Key topics to cover

  9. Generate and review

  10. Create script
  11. Edit as needed

docs.title: Result
docs.text: Podcast script ready for recording.
